prompt: A wide meadow at dusk photographed from a slightly elevated perspective. Across the grass stand several vertical piles of insect bodies arranged in a straight line across the landscape. Each pile is composed of hundreds of small insects: moths, beetles, flies, and other species stacked naturally into small mounds. The piles form a clear histogram-like pattern. On the left side of the meadow the piles are tall and dense, representing large insect populations in the past. Moving across the field from left to right, each pile becomes progressively smaller. Near the right side of the meadow the piles shrink to only a few scattered insects. At the far right the ground is almost empty, with a single living insect resting on the grass. The descending sequence of piles clearly communicates a dramatic collapse of insect populations across time. Photorealistic macro ecological photography, cinematic natural dusk lighting, highly detailed insects and grass textures, shallow depth of field. No text, numbers, charts, labels, or symbols.
dataset_name: A global database of long-term changes in insect assemblages
insight: A clear decline in insect populations across many study sites over time.
